Hit LOCATION (OPTIONAL RULE) In the standard combat rules, attacks hit various parts of the body; but the game makes no distinction between them_ The eftects of wounds are generalized represent the idea that some hit vital areas and others don"t The wound effects reflect general injury, without getting down to specifics of where each shot nits_ This optional rule allows players t0 work Out exactly where the target each attack lands. bringing greater detail for armor coverage and wound effects at the expense of simplicity: word 0f caution; while more realistic and descriptive tnan the standard abstract damage system; the use of hit Iocation can make the game much deadlier. Since few characters wear total-coverage armor; there will otten be attacks tnat strike unar- mored locations otherwise armored character; resulting more common wounding and death The gamemaster should be clear about this with the players, and hit Iocation should only be used everyone the game agrees.
